skill.name: cloud-devops
skill.description: Guides cloud and DevOps planning across infrastructure, containers, CI/CD, monitoring, security, cost optimization, and disaster recovery.
skill.summary: Coordinate cloud infrastructure and DevOps workflows from planning through operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many inline backtick expressions and several high-risk keywords, but review found they are Markdown skill names, copy-paste prompts, cloud service names, and workflow steps. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempt, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is low because the workflow advises users to invoke other skills and perform cloud operations that require normal human review.

low_findings.title: Static Command Pattern False Positive in Markdown
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md, but the file is a Markdown workflow guide. The backticks mark skill names, copy-paste prompts, and cloud service lists, not executable code. This is a false positive with low residual risk because users may still copy prompts that invoke other skills.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown bullets and fenced prompt examples. No shell interpreter, Ruby code, or command execution path appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Cloud Operations Guidance Requires Review
low_findings.description: The skill guides infrastructure provisioning, IAM setup, deployment, security assessment, and disaster recovery. These are legitimate DevOps activities, but mistakes can affect production systems if users apply generated steps without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly covers sensitive cloud operations, but it only provides planning guidance. The risk depends on user execution outside the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Backtick Markup Misclassified as Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Inline code spans and fenced Markdown blocks were misclassified as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Review found no executable code in the skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern exists syntactically, but the semantic context is Markdown formatting. There is no code runner or script body.

dangerous_patterns.title: High-Risk Keyword False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer flagged C2 and weak cryptography keywords, but the reviewed lines contain ordinary cloud workflow language such as architecture, Cloud Run, Cloud Functions, monitoring, and service names. No evidence found for command-and-control behavior or weak cryptographic implementation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is visible DevOps documentation and provider service naming. I found no encoded payloads, network endpoints, or cryptographic code.

content.value_statement: Cloud delivery work spans many tools, providers, and operational phases. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for planning infrastructure, deployments, monitoring, security, cost, and recovery.
actual_capabilities: Organizes cloud infrastructure setup into architecture, networking, resource provisioning, IAM, and billing steps.
Maps container work to Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, manifests, deployment, and networking activities.
Guides CI/CD planning with build automation, tests, deployment stages, rollback strategy, and notifications.
Covers monitoring and observability tasks for metrics, logs, tracing, dashboards, and alerts.
Includes security, cost optimization, and disaster recovery phases with quality gates.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an executable deployment tool.
It does not include provider-specific configuration files or commands.
It depends on separate referenced skills for detailed implementation work.
Generated plans still require review by cloud and security owners.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Cloud Platform
use_cases.description: Create a phased plan for architecture, accounts, networking, IAM, provisioning, and quality gates.

use_cases.title: Standardize Delivery Pipelines
use_cases.description: Define CI/CD stages, automated tests, rollback handling, and deployment notifications across services.

use_cases.title: Prepare Operational Readiness
use_cases.description: Build checklists for monitoring, security controls, cost management, incident response, and disaster recovery.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic DevOps Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new application
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cloud-devops workflow to create a phased DevOps plan for a new web application. Include infrastructure, deployment, monitoring, security, and recovery tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Kubernetes Delivery Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cloud-devops workflow to plan a Kubernetes deployment process for my service. Include Docker, manifests, Helm, networking, security policies, and rollout checks.

prompt_templates.title: Assess CI/CD and Observability Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cloud-devops workflow to review our current CI/CD and observability setup. Identify missing stages, alerts, dashboards, rollback steps, and quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Cloud Readiness Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cloud-devops workflow to create a multi-cloud readiness roadmap across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Cover governance, IaC, Kubernetes, CI/CD, monitoring, security, cost, and disaster recovery.

output_examples.input: Plan DevOps work for a new SaaS product on AWS.
output_examples.output: A phased delivery plan covering architecture, accounts, networking, Terraform provisioning, IAM, CI/CD stages, monitoring, security controls, cost alerts, and disaster recovery checks.

output_examples.input: Improve operations for an existing Kubernetes service.
output_examples.output: A prioritized checklist for container image hygiene, manifests, Helm packaging, deployment strategy, network policy, observability, alerts, rollback, and incident runbooks.

output_examples.input: Prepare a cloud platform quality gate review.
output_examples.output: A review outline that checks infrastructure readiness, pipeline health, monitoring coverage, security measures, cost optimization, and documented recovery procedures.

best_practices: Validate generated plans with cloud, security, and operations owners before applying changes.
Use least privilege, isolated environments, and staged rollouts for infrastructure and deployment work.
Convert workflow outputs into tracked tasks with owners, environments, dependencies, and acceptance checks.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated infrastructure changes directly against production without review.
Do not treat referenced skills as replacements for provider documentation or security policy.
Do not skip rollback, monitoring, and disaster recovery tasks to accelerate deployment.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy cloud infrastructure automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and planning prompts. Users must implement and review any deployment changes separately.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does it cover?
faq.answer: It references AWS, Azure, and GCP workflows, plus Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, monitoring, and security activities.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production operations?
faq.answer: It can support production planning, but all outputs should be reviewed by qualified cloud and security teams.

faq.question: Does it include concrete Terraform or Kubernetes files?
faq.answer: No. It points users toward workflow phases and related skills rather than providing full configuration files.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The skill covers sensitive cloud operations, so users should apply least privilege, approvals, testing, and change control.
